input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **and** the Xcode Command Line Tools:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- After install, accept the license:
  ```
  sudo xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Version discovery:** MAUI docs specify minimum Xcode version per .NET release. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/get-started/installation).

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — grab the latest **LTS** or **STS** release (the site labels them clearly).
- Use the macOS **Arm64** installer if on Apple Silicon, **x64** for Intel.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet --version
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- **Discover available workloads:**
  ```
  dotnet workload search maui
  ```
- **Verify installation:**
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```
  You should see `maui`, `maui-ios`, `maui-android` (and others) listed.

## 4. Install Android SDK & Java (for Android)

You have two paths:

### Option A — Visual Studio Code + Android Studio (recommended)
- Install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio).
- Through Android Studio → **SDK Manager**, install:
  - Latest Android SDK Platform (e.g., API 35)
  - Android SDK Build-Tools
  - Android SDK Command-line Tools
  - Android Emulator + a system image
- Android Studio bundles a compatible **JDK**. Alternatively, install **Microsoft OpenJDK 17**:
  ```
  brew install --cask microsoft-openjdk@17
  ```

### Option B — Let the MAUI workload handle it
The `maui-android` workload installs the Android SDK and OpenJDK automatically to:
```
~/Library/Android/sdk          # Android SDK
~/.android/                    # AVD data
```
Verify with:
```bash
dotnet android sdk info
```

### Set environment variables (if using Android Studio path):
```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-17.jdk/Contents/Home"
```
Add these to your `~/.zshrc`.

## 5. Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + [C# Dev Kit](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.csdevkit) + [.NET MAUI extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui) | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (full MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> **Note:** Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code or Rider are the current recommended options.

## 6. Verify Everything Works

```bash
# Create a test project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for iOS (requires Xcode)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# Run on Android Emulator (start one first via Android Studio)
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

Replace `net9.0` with your SDK's target (check with `dotnet new maui --list` or look at the generated `.csproj`).

## 7. Quick Troubleshooting

| Symptom | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `xcode-select: error` | Re-run `xcode-select --install` or point to Xcode: `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Android SDK not found | Set `ANDROID_HOME` and restart terminal |
| Workload restore fails | Run `sudo dotnet workload repair` |
| Emulator won't start (Apple Silicon) | Use an **arm64-v8a** system image in AVD Manager |

---

**Version compatibility cheat sheet:** Always cross-reference the [.NET MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new/) for the matrix of supported .NET SDK ↔ Xcode ↔ Android API levels.
